  "findings": [
    "ADAS forward-facing camera was not calibrated following windshield replacement",
    "Vehicle released to customer in a non-compliant and potentially unsafe state",
    "Absence of dashboard warning lights does not guarantee proper ADAS sensor alignment"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Recall the vehicle immediately for mandatory static target calibration",
    "Subcontract the calibration to a qualified facility or dealer",
    "Do not operate the vehicle's active safety systems until calibration is complete"
  ],
  "rationale": "Releasing a vehicle without performing the OEM-mandated static ADAS calibration after a windshield replacement is a critical safety violation. Even without active dashboard warning lights, the camera's pitch, yaw, or roll may be misaligned, causing safety systems like Automatic Emergency Braking (AEB) or Lane Keep Assist (LKA) to malfunction or fail to deploy correctly. Customer decline does not absolve the shop of liability or the requirement to meet OEM safety standards.",
  "references": [
    "OEM Position Statement on ADAS Calibration after Glass Replacement",
    "I-CAR Best Practices for ADAS Calibration"
  ]
}
